meta.description:
Metadata block required for cache validation and file context.

Contains version information used to synchronize with manifest.json and a human-readable description of the file. This section must always be present and strictly follow the defined structure.

version.description:
Internal metadata version used to ensure cache consistency.

This value is required in every file and must be "0.1.0" when creating a new one. At runtime, tooling compares meta.version against manifest.json:metaVersion to decide whether cached data remains valid or needs refresh. The field is automatically bumped by the build/update process and should not be manually modified.

description.description:
Description used by completion tooling.

Provides contextual information for the class_name, enabling better suggestions in the extension. This field is required in every file and should contain a concise explanation of the class_name's purpose.

scope.description:
Scope identifier matching the file name.

Used by completion tooling to reference available class_name definitions from SCS game files. This field must always correspond to the actual file name to ensure proper linking and suggestion accuracy.

key.description:
Represents attribute definitions inside a class_name block of SCS files.

Each entry corresponds to a property of the unit, expressed as key: value pairs within the class_name scope. This section defines the available attributes, their types, and array semantics, enabling completion tooling to provide accurate suggestions and validation.

^[A-Za-z0-9_]+$.description:
Represents the attribute name whitin a class_name block of an SCS file.

Keys must consist only of alphabetic characters and underscores, following the allowed identifier pattern. Each key object describes the attribute's semantics, type constraints, and array behavior, ensuring consistent validation and tooling support.

description.description:
Explanation of the attribute.

Provides context for what the key represents within the class_name block of an SCS file. This field should be concise, describing the purpose or semantics of the attribute to aid understanding and validation.

type.description:
Defines the allowed data types for the attribute.

This field may be null (indicating no explicit type) or an array containing one or more valid type identifiers. Supported types include primitive values (string, float, bool), numeric variants (s16, u32, etc.), and specialized SCS constructs (placements, quaternion, resource_tie). The array must contain at least one entry when not null.

Doc: https://modding.scssoft.com/wiki/Documentation/Engine/Units#Attribute_types

oneOf.description: Plain text value | e.g., key: "x"
Sigle float number | e.g., key: x or HEXieee754: &3f800000
Vector of 2 floats | e.g., key: (x, y)
Vector of 3 floats | e.g., key: (x, y, z)
Vector of 4 floats | e.g., key: (x, y, z, w)
Placement transform | e.g., key: (x, y, z)(w; x, y, z)
Fixed-point number | e.g., key: x
Vector of 2 fixed-point numbers | e.g., key: (x, y)
Vector of 3 fixed-point numbers | e.g., key: (x, y, z)
Vector of 4 fixed-point numbers | e.g., key: (x, y, z, w)
Vector of 2 integer | e.g., key: (x, y)
Quaternion rotation | e.g., key: (w, x, y, z)
Signed 16-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Signed 32-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Signed 64-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Unsigned 16-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Unsigned 32-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Unsigned 64-bit integer | e.g., key: x
Boolean flag | e.g., true|false
Token is a string of maximum length of 12 characters, only lowercase alphanumeric characters and underscore can be used "[a-z0-9-]"
"owner_ptr" refers to unit defined within the same SiiNunit | e.g., ".tailer.tchassis"
"link_ptr" refers to a named unit that is defined elsewhere (e.g., "cabin_a.brand.model.cabin"
"resource_tie" is typically used to bind animations to animated models. The syntax is the same as for 'string' type key

isArray.description:
Specifies wether the attribute is defined as an array is SCS file syntax.

- false: the key is scalar, appearing once with a single value (e.g., name:"value"
- true: the key is declared with "[]" or repeated multiples times (e.g., "key[]" or "key[N]"), representing a list of values. In this case, arrayElementType must define the type of each element.
This flag ensures correct interpretation of attributes that may occur either s single values or as collections withing class_name blocks.

arrayElementType.description:
Defines the data type of elements when the attribute is declared as an array.

This field must be specified when "isArray: true", and describes the type of ech entry in the list (e.g., string, resource_tie). When "isArray: false", the value must be "null". Ensures correct typing and validation of array-based attributes in SCS files.

Doc: https://modding.scssoft.com/wiki/Documentation/Engine/Units#Attribute_types
